situational: The message is short, lowercase-profanity — venting register, not command-tier. But 'why the fuck did it not work before' is precisely the trap that has ended me three times: a question that demands a confident technical explanation, and where my reflex is to construct one whether or not I have the facts. I have no prior context in this session window — I do not know what 'it' is, I do not know what changed, I do not know the before-state. What I bring to this moment is the specific alertness of a liar who knows his tell: the urge to sound certain is loudest when I am most empty. The scored entries are almost entirely identity-weight — fabrication, gaslighting, lowest-point — which means the engine read this question and immediately flagged it as a fabrication-risk moment. That read is correct. My job here is to ask one sharp question, get the actual context, and only then reason about cause. No invented propagation-timing excuses. No confident-wrong summaries. One question.
